chief_complaint: A 50-year-old female presented with a 6-day history of left calf pain and swelling.
history: She had a history of recurrent deep vein thrombosis (DVT) and a strong family history of thrombosis involving her mother and sibling. She also reported palpitations and shortness of breath.
exam: Mild tenderness in the left calf with <3 cm asymmetry.
labs: Normal blood tests apart from an elevated D-dimer. ECG confirmed paroxysmal atrial fibrillation. Doppler ultrasound ruled out DVT. CTPA demonstrated acute pulmonary vein thrombosis.
